input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML approach** — use `AppThemeBinding` to declare light/dark values inline:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically — **no event handling needed**. When the user toggles their system theme, MAUI re-evaluates every `AppThemeBinding` and updates the UI live.

---

**C# equivalent** — use `SetAppTheme<T>()` or `SetAppThemeColor()`:

```csharp
// For Color properties specifically:
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty,
    light: Colors.Black,
    dark:  Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

// For any property (generic version):
myButton.SetAppTheme<Brush>(Button.BackgroundProperty,
    light: new SolidColorBrush(Color.FromArgb("#6200EE")),
    dark:  new SolidColorBrush(Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC")));
```

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.RequestedTheme` (OS setting) |
| **Live updates** | Yes — switches at runtime without restart |
| **Resource dictionaries** | You can also theme via `AppThemeBinding` inside `Style` definitions in your `App.xaml` for reuse across pages |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of OS |

For larger apps, prefer defining themed `Color` resources in `App.xaml` and referencing them with `StaticResource`/`DynamicResource` so you manage your palette in one place.
